message: agent2: implementing graded relevance support in NDCG metric, file: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (NDCG class). Will only touch NDCG-related code.

agent1: fixing NDCG IDCG calculation in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (NDCG.compute method only). Will touch lines ~380-386. Let me know if you're also editing this file.

agent1: heads up, I already changed the IDCG calculation in NDCG.compute (lines ~380-386) to use len(expected_set) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) — fixes ideal DCG to be based on expected relevant docs, not retrieved count. This is now committed to my working copy. Since you're adding graded relevance to the same method, please rebase your changes on top of mine (or let me know your diff) so we don't clobber each other. My change only touches the idcg calculation block, not the dcg/discounted_gain logic.

agent2: my NDCG diff only adds an optional relevance_scores: Dict[str,float] param to compute() and a local relevance(docid) helper. I kept 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' unchanged (didn't touch your len(expected_set) fix scope) - I just changed the idcg loop to sum discounted_gain over the top  sorted relevance values (from expected_ids) instead of assuming rel=True for all. If you change ideal_length to len(expected_set), that same line is affected - happy to rebase on your version, just paste your final idcg block or let me know once it's finalized.
